html:
Trophy Games Development A/S, 4H7.F / TGAMES.CO

Trophy Games is not a generic mobile-game lottery ticket anymore. It is a small Danish publisher turning a repeatable niche-management engine into a portfolio of owned simulation games: Airline Manager, Truck Manager, Farm Manager, and next animal/horse titles. The old football-manager portfolio is now a cash-cow side bucket, not the core growth story. The prior SEGA / Football Manager Mobile framing appears wrong and understates the quality of the current transition.

The inflection is visible. 2025 revenue rose 40% to DKK 126.5m, EBITDA rose 26% to DKK 31.1m, EBT rose 109% to DKK 19.0m, and Q4 revenue doubled year over year. The “Big 3” transport titles produced 67% of 2025 revenue. Then Q1 2026 revenue rose 93% to DKK 42.0m, while EBITDA still grew 29% despite a deliberate marketing step-up. Paying users rose 46% and transactions rose 70% even though installs fell 7%, which is exactly the mix shift a small F2P publisher wants: fewer low-value installs, more monetizing users.

The market is focused on the ugly optical margin compression. That is the opportunity. Q1 marketing spend jumped to 38% of revenue from 25% because Trophy is scaling Truck Manager and Farm Manager, and management says these user-acquisition cohorts are expected to break even within two quarters. If that is even roughly true, reported EBIT is temporarily understated by growth spend. On a mature run-rate, the company is not a 14% EBIT-margin business. It is closer to a 25% to 30% EBITDA-margin portfolio with optionality from new verticals.

The 2026 guidance is conservative to the point of being useful. Management maintained DKK 130m to 153m revenue and DKK 18m to 24m EBIT after a DKK 42m Q1. Even the top of guidance implies the next three quarters average only DKK 37m of revenue, below both Q4 2025 and Q1 2026. Guidance also excludes acquisitions and The Ranchers. That gives the stock two ways to win: management simply executes the existing transport portfolio above guidance, or the excluded publishing/animal pipeline contributes without being in numbers.

The product evidence supports the financials. Truck Manager has crossed 10m+ Google Play downloads with a 4.79 AndroidRank rating, while Farm Manager has 5m+ Google Play downloads and strong ratings. Farm Manager did DKK 12.6m of Q1 revenue after launching only in July 2025. Truck Manager revenue rose to DKK 10.4m in Q1 from DKK 2.1m a year earlier. Airline Manager remains the profit anchor. The three largest games were 80% of Q1 revenue, but the older “other titles” still generated about DKK 5.1m with essentially no marketing or development spend, which is valuable downside protection.

There is also underappreciated portfolio optionality. The Tivola horse-management game is structured with DKK 5.9m of German public co-financing against a DKK 13.2m budget, with Trophy recouping its investment first before revenue sharing. The Ranchers is not in guidance, and the demo’s review profile is mixed, but recent Steam sentiment improved from the weak launch base. I would not make The Ranchers the core thesis. It is a free call option sitting on top of a transport portfolio that is already working.

The stock is still ignored. Stockopedia shows no analyst target, StockAnalysis shows only about 29k average daily Copenhagen shares traded, and retail discussion is thin, mostly local forums and sparse Investing.com commentary. This is not a crowded “AI game publisher” story. It is an illiquid Nordic microcap with a founder-led team, net cash, no interest-bearing debt, and a now-proven ability to buy or build small niche games and scale them with paid acquisition.

At the May 15, 2026 German close, 4H7 trades around EUR 2.14. With about 26.5m shares, market cap is roughly EUR 57m, and after Q1 cash of DKK 38.3m with no interest-bearing debt, EV is about EUR 52m, or DKK 385m. On stale trailing EBIT that looks only fair. On current-cycle economics, if revenue lands around DKK 165m to 175m and normalized EBIT power moves toward DKK 35m to 45m as Q1 marketing cohorts season, the stock is closer to 9x to 11x normalized EBIT for a business growing revenue 25%+ with net cash.

The bear case is that paid acquisition stops working. Farm Manager proves front-loaded, Truck Manager retention decays, margins stay at the Q1 trough, and 2026 revenue merely hits guidance with EBIT stuck near DKK 18m. A thinly traded microcap then deserves a low multiple. Bear target: EUR 1.20, down 44%.

The base case is that guidance is too low, Q1 was not a fluke, and the transport portfolio exits 2026 at a DKK 170m+ revenue run-rate with EBIT power above DKK 35m. A 15x to 16x forward EBIT multiple plus cash supports a materially higher equity value. Base target: EUR 3.50, up 64%.

The bull case is that Trophy has found a repeatable niche-sim factory. Truck and Farm continue scaling, Airline remains a high-return cash generator, animal/horse expands the addressable portfolio, and The Ranchers or publishing adds incremental upside. At DKK 220m+ revenue and DKK 55m to 65m normalized EBIT, a strategic buyer or public market could pay 18x to 20x EBIT. Bull target: EUR 6.00, up 180%.
